conditionA_Baseline.name: Register Loss Information
conditionA_Baseline.summary: A Clerk captures and registers loss information in the claims-capturing computer system, including policy and incident details, while the System validates data, assigns a claim number, and retrieves coverage information.

steps.description: Begins loss entry in the claims-capturing system.
Enters available policy and incident details into the system.
Asynchronously validates entered data.
Matches the loss to a policy.
Checks for duplicate claims.
Assigns a claim number.
Retrieves related coverage information.
Supports adjuster assignment.
Confirms completion of the loss information entry.
Stores the loss information.
Triggers acknowledgment actions.
Ensures the claim is ready for further processing.

loops.condition: While the Clerk enters details and until sufficient information is provided and confirmed

conditionB_EnhancedHITL.name: Register Loss Information
conditionB_EnhancedHITL.summary: A Clerk captures and registers loss information in the claims-capturing computer system, including policy and incident details, while the System validates data, assigns a claim number, and retrieves coverage information.

steps.description: Begins loss entry in the claims-capturing system.
Enters available policy and incident details into the system.
Asynchronously validates entered data.
Matches the loss to a policy.
Checks for duplicate claims.
Assigns a claim number.
Retrieves related coverage information.
Supports adjuster assignment.
Confirms completion of the loss information entry.
Stores the loss information.
Triggers acknowledgment actions.
Ensures the claim is ready for further processing.

flows.condition: System identifies potential duplicate claims

steps.description: Reviews potential duplicates presented by the System.
Makes a decision regarding the duplicate claims.

flows.condition: Data submitted in step 2 (policy number/name and date of incident) is incomplete or invalid, and the System does not find a match

steps.description: Retries with different search details or corrects the policy number/name.

flows.condition: The loss is 'If not found' or 'If mismatched'

steps.description: Re-enters or corrects the information as required by the System.

flows.condition: Asynchronous validation in step 3 fails

steps.description: Highlights invalid data fields to the Clerk with specific error messages.
Corrects the flagged data.

flows.condition: Data submitted in step 2 is incomplete, invalid, or missing required fields

steps.description: Blocks the search functionality or returns a 'no match found' result.
Reviews and corrects the input (Policy number/name, date of incident) to meet system requirements.

flows.condition: No suitable adjusters are available at Step 6

steps.description: Notifies the Clerk that no suitable adjusters are available.
Saves the claim for later assignment.

flows.condition: No suitable adjusters are available at Step 6, and a timeout or critical service level agreement is approaching

steps.description: Notifies the Clerk that no suitable adjusters are available.
Assigns a default adjuster, especially if a timeout or critical service level agreement is approaching.

flows.condition: The validation in step 5 fails, indicating that the basic loss information entered by the Clerk is invalid or incomplete.

steps.description: Displays a warning to the Clerk highlighting specific fields that need correction or completion.
Corrects the invalid data.
Validates the basic information again.

flows.condition: No suitable adjusters are available at Step 6, and the Clerk chooses to save the claim for later assignment.

steps.description: Notifies the Clerk that no suitable adjusters are available.
Saves the claim for later assignment.

flows.condition: No suitable adjusters are available at Step 6, and a timeout occurs or pre-defined escalation rules trigger an automatic assignment.

steps.description: Notifies the Clerk that no suitable adjusters are available.
Automatically assigns a default adjuster if a specific timeout occurs or based on pre-defined escalation rules.

flows.condition: No suitable adjusters are available during adjuster assignment (Step 6), and the Clerk chooses to save the claim for later assignment.

steps.description: Notifies the Clerk that no suitable adjusters are available during adjuster assignment.
Saves the claim for later assignment.

flows.condition: No suitable adjusters are available during adjuster assignment (Step 6), and a timeout scenario occurs.

steps.description: Notifies the Clerk that no suitable adjusters are available during adjuster assignment.
Assigns a default adjuster.

flows.condition: The final validation in Step 12 fails, meaning minimum required information (Date of Loss, Insured Name, Policy #, Adjuster) is missing.

steps.description: Warns the Clerk that minimum required information (Date of Loss, Insured Name, Policy #, Adjuster) is missing.
Prevents the Clerk from exiting the claim or discards unsaved changes if the Clerk insists on exiting despite the warning.
Corrects the missing information.

flows.condition: No suitable adjusters are available when the Clerk attempts to assign one (branch from MAIN step 8).

steps.description: Notifies the Clerk that no suitable adjusters are available.
Saves the claim for later assignment.

flows.condition: No suitable adjusters are available when the Clerk attempts to assign one, and a timeout scenario occurs (branch from MAIN step 8).

steps.description: Notifies the Clerk that no suitable adjusters are available.
Automatically assigns a default adjuster due to a timeout scenario.

flows.condition: Final validation after Clerk confirms completion (step 10) fails due to missing minimum required information (Date of Loss, Insured Name, Policy #, Adjuster).

steps.description: Warns the Clerk that minimum required information (Date of Loss, Insured Name, Policy #, Adjuster) is missing.
Prevents the Clerk from exiting the claim without correcting the data.

flows.condition: The Clerk insists on exiting despite the warning, from EXT_10a, step 2.

steps.description: Discards unsaved changes if the Clerk insists on exiting despite the warning.

flows.condition: No suitable adjusters are available when the Clerk attempts to assign one

steps.description: Notifies the Clerk that no suitable adjusters are available.
Saves the claim for later assignment.

flows.condition: No suitable adjusters are available when the Clerk attempts to assign one, and a timeout scenario occurs

steps.description: Notifies the Clerk that no suitable adjusters are available.
Automatically assigns a default adjuster, particularly in timeout scenarios.

flows.condition: The final validation fails due to missing minimum required information (Date of Loss, Insured Name, Policy #, Adjuster)

steps.description: Warns the Clerk that minimum required information (Date of Loss, Insured Name, Policy #, Adjuster) is missing.
Prevents the Clerk from exiting the claim processing.

flows.condition: The Clerk insists on exiting without completing the required fields

steps.description: Discards unsaved changes.

loops.condition: While the Clerk enters details and until sufficient information is provided and confirmed

questions: How specifically is "Asynchronously validates entered data." performed in step 3? What are the detailed actions?
How specifically is "Checks for duplicate claims." performed in step 5? What are the detailed actions?
What happens if the data submitted in step 2 is incomplete, invalid, or missing required fields?
What happens if the validation in step 3 fails? (Asynchronously validates entered data.)
What happens if the data submitted in step 2 is incomplete, invalid, or missing required fields?
What happens if no resources (agents, slots, capacity) are available at step 6?
answers: In step 3, 'Asynchronously validates entered data' likely refers to the System performing real-time checks on the loss information entered by the Clerk. This includes format validation (e.g., date formats), range checks (e.g., policy start/end dates), and logical consistency checks (e.g., date of loss cannot be in the future). The validation occurs in the background as the Clerk enters data, providing immediate feedback.

In step 5, 'Checks for duplicate claims' involves the System comparing newly entered basic loss information (e.g., Insured Name, Date of Loss, Policy Number, type of incident) against existing claims in the database. The System uses a combination of fuzzy matching algorithms and direct data comparison to identify claims with similar characteristics. It then presents a list of potential duplicates to the Clerk for review and decision.

If the data submitted in step 2 (policy number/name and date of incident) is incomplete or invalid, the System will not find a match. The Clerk must then retry with different search details or correct the policy number/name. This scenario is explicitly handled in Phase 1 as 'If not found' or 'If mismatched', requiring the Clerk to re-enter or correct the information.

If the asynchronous validation in Step 3 fails, the System would immediately highlight the invalid data fields to the Clerk with specific error messages. The Clerk would then be required to correct the flagged data before being allowed to proceed with further claim processing. This ensures data integrity before the claim progresses to adjuster assignment.

If the data submitted in Step 2 is incomplete, invalid, or missing required fields, the System will either block the search functionality until valid data is entered or return a 'no match found' result. The Clerk would then be prompted to review and correct the input (Policy number/name, date of incident) to meet the system's requirements before attempting to identify a policy again. This is covered within Phase 1's validation loops.

If no suitable adjusters are available at Step 6, the System will notify the Clerk. The Clerk then has the option to save the claim for later assignment, or the System may assign a default adjuster, especially if a timeout or critical service level agreement is approaching. This allows the claim to be created even if immediate appropriate assignment is not possible.

questions: What happens if the validation in step 5 fails? (Checks for duplicate claims.)
What happens if no resources (agents, slots, capacity) are available at step 6?
answers: If the validation in step 5 fails, it implies that the basic loss information entered by the Clerk is invalid or incomplete. The System would then display a warning message to the Clerk, highlighting the specific fields that need correction or completion. The Clerk must then correct the invalid data to proceed with the claim registration. The flow continues only after the basic information is successfully validated again.

If no suitable adjusters are available at Step 6, the System notifies the Clerk. The Clerk is then given the option to either save the claim for later assignment, allowing them to return and assign an adjuster once one becomes available, or the System may automatically assign a default adjuster if a specific timeout occurs or based on pre-defined escalation rules. The process can then continue with the default assignment or be resumed later.

questions: What happens if no resources (agents, slots, capacity) are available at step 6?
What happens if the validation in step 12 fails? (Ensures the claim is ready for further processing.)
answers: This scenario is explicitly covered in the detailed description. If no suitable adjusters are available during the adjuster assignment (Step 6), the System notifies the Clerk. The Clerk then has the option to save the claim for later assignment or the System may assign a default adjuster, especially if a timeout scenario occurs. This allows the process to continue or be deferred.

This scenario is explicitly covered in the detailed description. If the final validation in Step 12 fails, meaning minimum required information (Date of Loss, Insured Name, Policy #, Adjuster) is missing, the System warns the Clerk. The System may prevent the Clerk from exiting the claim or discard unsaved changes if the Clerk insists on exiting despite the warning. The Clerk must then correct the missing information to proceed.

questions: What happens if no resources (agents, slots, capacity) are available at step 6?
What happens if the validation in step 9 fails? (Confirms completion of the loss information entry.)
answers: This scenario is triggered when the Clerk attempts to assign an adjuster, and the System indicates that no suitable adjusters are available. The System notifies the Clerk of the unavailability. The Clerk then has the option to save the claim for later assignment, or the System may automatically assign a default adjuster, particularly in timeout scenarios.

This scenario is triggered when the Clerk confirms completion, but the System's final validation check for minimum required information (Date of Loss, Insured Name, Policy #, Adjuster) fails. The System warns the Clerk about the missing information. The System may prevent the Clerk from exiting the claim without correcting the missing data, or may discard unsaved changes if the Clerk insists on exiting despite the warning.

questions: What happens if no resources (agents, slots, capacity) are available at step 6?
What happens if the validation in step 9 fails? (Confirms completion of the loss information entry.)
answers: This scenario is explicitly covered in the description. If no suitable adjusters are available, the System notifies the Clerk. The Clerk then has the option to save the claim for later assignment, or the System may automatically assign a default adjuster, particularly in timeout scenarios.

This scenario is explicitly covered in the description. If the final validation fails due to missing minimum required information (Date of Loss, Insured Name, Policy #, Adjuster), the System warns the Clerk. The System may prevent exiting the claim processing or discard unsaved changes if the Clerk insists on exiting without completing the required fields.

groundTruth.name: Register Loss
groundTruth.summary: Clerk captures loss information in claims system through flexible data entry, with system validation, duplicate detection, and automatic saving
postconditions: Loss information is captured and stored

steps.description: Enters insured's policy number or name and date of incident
Populates available policy information and indicates claim is matched to policy
Enters basic loss information
Confirms no existing competing claims and assigns claim number
Continues entering loss information specific to claim line
Has System pull other coverage information from other computer systems
Selects and assigns an adjuster
Confirms they are finished
Saves loss and triggers acknowledgement to be sent to agent

flows.condition: Power failure during loss capture

steps.description: Autosaves intermittently at transaction commit points

flows.condition: Claim is not for our company to handle

steps.description: Indicates claim is entered only for recording purposes and continues or ends loss

flows.condition: Found policy information does not match the insured's information

steps.description: Enters correct policy number or insured name and asks System to populate with new policy index information

flows.condition: Using search details, System could not find a policy

steps.description: Returns to loss and enters available data

flows.condition: Clerk changed policy number, date of loss or claim line after initial policy match

steps.description: Validates changes, populates loss with correct policy information, validates and indicates claim is matched to policy
Warns Clerk to re-evaluate coverage

flows.condition: System cannot validate policy match

steps.description: Warns Clerk
Finds the policy using the search details for policy

flows.condition: Clerk wants to restart a loss which has been interrupted, saved or needs completion

steps.description: Finds a loss using search details for loss
Opens it for editing

flows.condition: Clerk changes claim line previously entered and no line specific data has been entered

steps.description: Presents appropriate line specific sections of loss based on new claim line

flows.condition: Clerk changes claim line previously entered and there is data in some line specific fields

steps.description: Warns that data exists and asks Clerk to either cancel changes or proceed with new claim line

flows.condition: Clerk cancels change

steps.description: Continues with the loss

flows.condition: Clerk insists on new claim line

steps.description: Blanks out line specific data but keeps all basic claim level data

flows.condition: System detects possible duplicate claim

steps.description: Displays a list of possible duplicate claims from loss database
Selects and views a claim from the list

flows.condition: Clerk finds that the claim is a duplicate

steps.description: Opens duplicate claim from list for editing if not yet marked completed and may delete previously saved data

flows.condition: Clerk finds that the claim is not a duplicate

steps.description: Returns to loss and completes it

flows.condition: Preliminary loss information is changed after initial duplicate claim check is done

steps.description: Performs duplicate claim check again

flows.condition: Clerk needs to save loss before completion

steps.description: Has System save the loss for completion at a later time

flows.condition: Claim line or loss description are changed after coverage was reviewed

steps.description: Warns Clerk to re-evaluate coverage

flows.condition: Clerk confirms finished without completing minimum information

steps.description: Warns Clerk it cannot accept loss without date of loss, insured name or policy number and handling adjuster

flows.condition: Clerk decides to continue or save without marking complete

steps.description: Continues entering loss or saves without marking complete

flows.condition: Clerk insists on exiting without entering minimum information

steps.description: Discards any intermediate saves and exits

flows.condition: Missing required fields for claim number assignment

steps.description: Warns Clerk it cannot assign claim number without required fields and directs to fields requiring entry

flows.condition: Timeout: System decides to commit but handling adjuster not entered

steps.description: Assigns default adjuster
